free_text: Measurement of the cross section for the
13C(alpha,n)16O reaction and determination of the cross
section for the 16O(n,alpha)13C reaction
Data for O-16(n,alpha) reaction is given
IPPE 3-MV tandem accelerator
The neutrons were registered by a detector
with a 40 x 40 mm p-terphenyl single crystal. The
flight path between the target and the neutron
detector amounted to 71.3+ - 0.4 cm for the first
experimental run and to 65.2+ -0.4cm for the second
one.
The total target thickness was ranging from 35 keV at
6.2 Mev to 72 keV at 2.0 MeV. The thickness of 13C
targets was determined by nuclear reaction analysis
through the 13C(d,p0)14C and 13C(d,alpha0)11B
reactions. The number of 13C nuclei per unit target
area thus obtained appeared to be (2.20+ -
0.09)*10**(18) atoms/cm2 for the gold backing target
and (2.25+ -0.09)*10**(18) atoms/cm2 for the
molybdenum backing target.
